| Founded | 1891 |
| Dissolved | 1940 |
| Ground | [Portland Park](/wiki/Portland_Park,_Galston "Portland Park, Galston"), [Galston](/wiki/Galston,_East_Ayrshire "Galston, East Ayrshire") |

**Galston Football Club** were a [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club based in [Galston](/wiki/Galston,_East_Ayrshire "Galston, East Ayrshire"), [Ayrshire](/wiki/Ayrshire "Ayrshire"), [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland").[[3]](#cite_note-3) The club were members of the [Scottish Football League Third Division](/wiki/Scottish_Football_League_Third_Division "Scottish Football League Third Division") and played at [Portland Park](/wiki/Portland_Park,_Galston "Portland Park, Galston").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Galston_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Formed in 1891 to play in the new [Ayrshire Football League](/wiki/Ayrshire_Football_League "Ayrshire Football League"), they lasted in that competition for its four-year run before joining the [Ayrshire Football Combination](/wiki/Ayrshire_Football_Combination "Ayrshire Football Combination"). After three years without regular competition the club joined the North Ayrshire League, later appearing in the Scottish Football Combination and [Scottish Football Union](/wiki/Scottish_Football_Union "Scottish Football Union").[[4]](#cite_note-kit-4)

They eventually joined the Western league, which was incorporated by the [Scottish Football League](/wiki/Scottish_Football_League "Scottish Football League") as its new Third Division for the [1923–24 season](/wiki/1923%E2%80%9324_Scottish_Football_League "1923–24 Scottish Football League").[[4]](#cite_note-kit-4) Galston lasted the Division's three seasons, finishing 13th, 11th and 16th, although they resigned midway through the abandoned final season, unable to provide match guarantees.[[4]](#cite_note-kit-4) Indeed, Galston had been so keen to increase the income from their meagre gates that they were censured by the League for charging entrance fees below the minimum agreed league level, then in turn blamed for accelerating the collapse of the Third Division by being the first to pull out from it in February 1926.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

They proved one of the division's survivors and found themselves playing in first the West of Scotland Amateur league before being invited to join the Scottish Football Alliance from 1932 onward – a league made up of Senior Reserve teams and non-league sides which they had been invited to join to generate more interest in a competition many Senior clubs were considering abandoning due to a lack of public interest.[[4]](#cite_note-kit-4)

However, in 1938, [Hamilton Academical](/wiki/Hamilton_Academical_F.C. "Hamilton Academical F.C.") proposed they and [Beith](/wiki/Beith_F.C. "Beith F.C.") be expelled and the league restricted to First Division clubs reserve teams only. Despite an impassioned plea by letter from club President James Abbott to every Scottish League side – pointing out they had brought Beith and Galston in to prop up the league in the first place – only [Ayr United](/wiki/Ayr_United_F.C. "Ayr United F.C."), [Clyde](/wiki/Clyde_F.C. "Clyde F.C."), [Kilmarnock](/wiki/Kilmarnock_F.C. "Kilmarnock F.C."), [Queen's Park](/wiki/Queen%27s_Park_F.C. "Queen's Park F.C.") and [St Mirren](/wiki/St_Mirren_F.C. "St Mirren F.C.") voted to retain them and both sides were duly expelled.[[4]](#cite_note-kit-4)

The Alliance was then abandoned and a new Reserve League set up, whereupon moves were made in 1940 for a new Scottish Football Alliance to be set up, among which the invitees were Galston and [Vale of Leven](/wiki/Vale_Of_Leven_F.C. "Vale Of Leven F.C."), another side that had been damaged by the collapse of the Third Division and who had not played competitively since 1929 over a lack of any Senior league in which to play.[[4]](#cite_note-kit-4) However, after only a month's worth of games the [Second World War](/wiki/Second_World_War "Second World War") broke out and the league was put on indefinite hold. Without any Senior league to play in, and not interested in joining the [Junior ranks](/wiki/Scottish_Junior_Football_Association "Scottish Junior Football Association"), this marked the end of Galston FC.[[4]](#cite_note-kit-4)

## Kit

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Galston_F.C.&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Kit")]

During their relatively brief history Galston wore a number of different kits. They can be summarised thus:[[4]](#cite_note-kit-4)

## Scottish Cup record

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Galston_F.C.&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Scottish Cup record")]

During the course of their existence Galston qualified for the [Scottish Cup](/wiki/Scottish_Cup "Scottish Cup") eighteen times. They made the second round on seven occasions ([1906–07](/wiki/1906%E2%80%9307_Scottish_Cup "1906–07 Scottish Cup"), [1907–08](/wiki/1907%E2%80%9308_Scottish_Cup "1907–08 Scottish Cup"), [1910–11](/wiki/1910%E2%80%9311_Scottish_Cup "1910–11 Scottish Cup"), [1922–23](/wiki/1922%E2%80%9323_Scottish_Cup "1922–23 Scottish Cup"), [1923–24](/wiki/1923%E2%80%9324_Scottish_Cup "1923–24 Scottish Cup"), [1933–34](/wiki/1933%E2%80%9334_Scottish_Cup "1933–34 Scottish Cup") and [1935–36](/wiki/1935%E2%80%9336_Scottish_Cup "1935–36 Scottish Cup")), going out in the first round eleven times ([1894–95](/wiki/1894%E2%80%9395_Scottish_Cup "1894–95 Scottish Cup"), [1899–1900](/wiki/1899%E2%80%931900_Scottish_Cup "1899–1900 Scottish Cup"), [1905–06](/wiki/1905%E2%80%9306_Scottish_Cup "1905–06 Scottish Cup"), [1919–20](/wiki/1919%E2%80%9320_Scottish_Cup "1919–20 Scottish Cup"), [1920–21](/wiki/1920%E2%80%9321_Scottish_Cup "1920–21 Scottish Cup"), [1924–25](/wiki/1924%E2%80%9325_Scottish_Cup "1924–25 Scottish Cup"), [1928–29](/wiki/1928%E2%80%9329_Scottish_Cup "1928–29 Scottish Cup"), [1929–30](/wiki/1929%E2%80%9330_Scottish_Cup "1929–30 Scottish Cup"), [1934–35](/wiki/1934%E2%80%9335_Scottish_Cup "1934–35 Scottish Cup"), [1936–37](/wiki/1936%E2%80%9337_Scottish_Cup "1936–37 Scottish Cup"), [1937–38](/wiki/1937%E2%80%9338_Scottish_Cup "1937–38 Scottish Cup")).[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Football in the town

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Galston_F.C.&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Football in the town")]

The town of Galston has been represented at amateur level since the early 1970s by *Galston United* who play in the upper echelons of the [local amateur league](/wiki/Ayrshire_Amateur_Football_Association "Ayrshire Amateur Football Association");[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) they play at the local secondary school ([Loudoun Academy](/wiki/Loudoun_Academy "Loudoun Academy")) and have no link to the original club.
